title: wollastonia subcordata
intro: Wollastonia subcordata, also known as Philippine thistle or Chinese thistle, is a plant species native to Asia and Australia, characterized by its prickly, thistle-like leaves and purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Wollastonia subcordata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat digestive problems, making it a valuable remedy in folk medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
